title: Bridge Essay: Mistaking imagination for reality: Congruent mental imagery leads to more libera

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Imaginal bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**Mistaking imagination for reality: Congruent mental imagery leads to more liberal perceptual detection.** — Cognition (2021). PMID: 33878636. DOI: 10.1016/j.cognition.2021.104719. Score: 64. Visual experiences can be triggered externally, by signals coming from the outside world during perception; or internally, by signals from memory during mental imagery. Imagery and perception activate similar neural codes in sensory areas, suggesting that they might sometimes be confused. In the current study, we investigated whether imagery influences perception by instructing participants to imagine gratings while externally detecting these same gratings at threshold.
